raw_text_excerpt: From the Pricing page (claude.com/pricing#api → platform.claude.com/docs/en/docs/about-claude/pricing) model-pricing table verbatim: 'Claude Opus 4.7 | $5 / MTok | $6.25 / MTok | $10 / MTok | $0.50 / MTok | $25 / MTok'; 'Claude Sonnet 4.6 | $3 / MTok | $3.75 / MTok | $6 / MTok | $0.30 / MTok | $15 / MTok'; 'Claude Haiku 4.5 | $1 / MTok | $1.25 / MTok | $2 / MTok | $0.10 / MTok | $5 / MTok'. Columns are: Base Input Tokens | 5m Cache Writes | 1h Cache Writes | Cache Hits & Refreshes | Output Tokens. Corroborated on the Models Overview page (platform.claude.com/docs/en/docs/about-claude/models/overview) latest-models-comparison table 'Pricing' row: 'Claude Opus 4.7: $5 / input MTok, $25 / output MTok'; 'Claude Sonnet 4.6: $3 / input MTok, $15 / output MTok'; 'Claude Haiku 4.5: $1 / input MTok, $5 / output MTok'. Legacy-models table shows Claude Opus 4.1 + Claude Opus 4 (deprecated) at the older $15 input / $75 output rate, consistent with the M10 attestation epoch.
notes: Pricing attestation archived at M11b Commit A per pre-reg ~/.claude/plans/m11b-cross-model-sweep.md §D4 HARD GATE. Three findings: (1) Claude Sonnet 4.6 rate $3 input / $15 output matches the plan §D4 placeholder verbatim — no plan update needed. (2) Claude Haiku 4.5 rate $1 input / $5 output matches the plan §D4 placeholder verbatim — no plan update needed. (3) Claude Opus 4.7 rate observed at $5 input / $25 output does NOT match the M10 lock of $15 input / $75 output (agent/arbiter.py:15-16 per M10 attestation SHA 68d42e3, 2026-04-27). The Opus rate has rotated ~3x cheaper on input + ~3x cheaper on output in the ~2.5 weeks since the M10 attestation epoch. The published table shows Claude Opus 4.6 + Claude Opus 4.5 also at the $5 / $25 rate, indicating an Opus 4.6-generation re-pricing rather than a Claude-Opus-4-7-specific rate change. Per pre-reg §D4 point 3 + point 5 (locked at Commit A): the M10 lock HOLDS for all M11b cost computations to preserve cross-milestone consistency with M10/M10b/M11a cost numbers in runs/17,18,19. M11b internal cost-modeling rates are recorded above in the separate m11b_internal_cost_modeling_rates_per_million_tokens field for transparency; agent/arbiter.py OPUS_INPUT_USD_PER_M = 15.0 and OPUS_OUTPUT_USD_PER_M = 75.0 remain unchanged through Commit B. The Commit B rate-table refactor adds claude-sonnet-4-6 + claude-haiku-4-5 constants at the observed-published rates and routes through agent.arbiter._rates_for(model) substring dispatch; the Opus path remains bit-identical to M10/M10b/M11a (verified at Commit B by the 3-cell V2-Opus carryover smoke vs runs/data/17b-content-opus-v2-*.json). Cross-tier Pareto ratios reported in this paper (V2-Opus vs poll-Opus, V2-Sonnet vs poll-Sonnet, V2-Haiku vs poll-Haiku, cross-tier V2-Sonnet/Haiku vs V2-Opus, etc.) are insensitive to a uniform Opus-rate scaling factor — re-pricing Opus to $5/$25 would scale all Opus-tier absolute $-per-hit numbers by 1/3 but leave the matched-arbiter and cross-tier ratios unchanged at the per-trace level. Absolute Opus-tier $/hit numbers in this paper should be divided by ~3 for current-Anthropic-rate deployment cost projections. The Commit D analysis appendix at runs/20-cross-model-sweep.md reproduces this caveat in the per-tier Pareto-cost table footer.
